one_line_summary: ROS 2-based autonomous robot system for object collection using mecanum drive, computer vision, and a 5-DOF xArm manipulator.
audience: Student engineers and researchers working on autonomous robotics. Users need basic ROS 2 knowledge but the documentation should avoid jargon.
primary_workflows: Full system bringup and autonomous collection

components.name: system_manager_package
components.purpose: Central FSM orchestrator and GUI tools for the robot collection pipeline

components.name: wskr
components.purpose: Whisker-based navigation using fisheye camera for proximity sensing and autopilot control

components.name: wskr_search_behavior
components.purpose: Search and wander behavior for finding objects using vision or ArUco markers

components.name: xarm_object_collector_package
components.purpose: xArm 5-DOF manipulator control with genetic algorithm trajectory planning

components.name: vision_processing_package
components.purpose: YOLO-based object detection and tracking with TensorRT inference

components.name: gstreamer_camera
components.purpose: Camera driver using GStreamer pipeline for JPEG image publishing

components.name: arduino
components.purpose: Serial bridge to Arduino for mecanum wheel motor control

components.name: utilities
components.purpose: Debugging and tuning GUIs for robot development

data_flow_notes: The robot operates as a finite state machine (FSM) managed by StateManagerNode. States flow: IDLE -> SEARCH -> SELECT -> APPROACH_OBJ -> GRASP -> FIND_BOX -> APPROACH_BOX -> DROP -> SEARCH (loop). WSKR nodes provide navigation: floor detection creates a mask, range node casts 11 whisker rays for proximity, dead reckoning fuses visual + odometry heading, autopilot uses MLP to convert whiskers+heading to cmd_vel. Vision pipeline: gstreamer_camera publishes images, process_object_vision runs YOLO inference, object_selection picks best target. xArm nodes handle grasping via genetic algorithm trajectory planning. All communication via ROS 2 topics/actions/services.
